name: Remote: FTP Detected on Non-Standard Port
notes: This rule triggers when a remote FTP communication is observed on a non-standard port. The default port for FTP is TCP port 21.  Detecting FTP on other ports may indicate an exploited host, where the attacker has installed this service to provide backdoor access to the host.
conditions_text: when the flow context is Local to Local
when the flow matches Application is DataTransfer.FTP
when a flow matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication
when the destination port is one of the following 21, 20
TD_list: APPLY Remote: FTP Detected on Non-Standard Port on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND NOT when the flow context is Local to Local
AND when the flow matches Application is DataTransfer.FTP
AND when a flow matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication
AND NOT when the destination port is one of the following 21, 20
RED_list: Event name: Remote Hidden FTP Server

Event description: Detects a remote FTP server on a non-standard port.  The default port for FTP is TCP port 21.  Detecting FTP on other ports may indicate an exploited host, where the attacker has installed this server to provide backdoor access to the host.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
